title: Skill audit: emit mechanism descriptions, not just category verdicts
description: Mira's soul_manager.audit_skill() currently flags dangerous patterns and returns a pass/fail verdict with a category label (e.g. 'suspicious network request', 'dangerous exec call'). The reading note's core insight is that category descriptions are epistemically insufficient — 'will write exploits' tells you nothing about trigger conditions, propagation paths, or what specifically makes something dangerous. The same gap exists in Mira's skill audit: if a skill is blocked, the log says WHAT category was violated but not WHERE in the skill, WHICH pattern triggered it, or HOW the mechanism could cause harm. Change audit_skill() to return structured findings: for each flagged item, include (a) the matched line number and raw line content, (b) the specific regex/pattern that triggered, and (c) a one-sentence mechanism description of why that pattern is dangerous. Log this to the skill audit log. This turns the audit from a category gate into a mechanism record.
rationale: The reading note argues: 'controlled evaluation that can only produce category descriptions, not mechanism descriptions — what does controlled mean?' Mira's audit_skill() is exactly this: it produces category-level verdicts. A skill flagged for 'obfuscated code' gives no insight into what the obfuscation does, what it decodes to, or under what call sequence it activates. Category descriptions protect against known-bad patterns but cannot support reasoning about novel or edge-case skill behavior. Mechanism descriptions — line-level, pattern-specific, causally annotated — are what make the audit's 'controlled' claim meaningful.
diff_description: In soul_manager.audit_skill(), replace the current boolean/string verdict return with a structured result dict: {'passed': bool, 'findings': [{'line_no': int, 'line_content': str, 'pattern': str, 'category': str, 'mechanism': str}]}. For each compiled regex check (network requests, exec/eval, obfuscation, privilege escalation), when a match is found, capture match.group(0), the line number via enumerate, and append a static mechanism string per pattern (e.g. for curl-pipe-to-shell: 'downloads and executes arbitrary remote code in the agent process'). Update the caller sites to log findings at WARNING level with line content and mechanism, not just category. No change to blocking logic — only the evidence surfaced changes.
